title: Norantea cuneifolia
intro: Norantea cuneifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical forests of Madagascar. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its reported anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. Bioactive constituents of Norantea cuneifolia include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Norantea cuneifolia include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to promote relaxation, relieve pain, and support immune function.
taxonomy: Norantea cuneifolia is a species within the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Norantea. The classification of Norantea cuneifolia is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Gentianales, Family: Rubiaceae, Genus: Norantea, Species: N. cuneifolia. This species is native to Madagascar.
